rag_rules: Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for branching logic and implications
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to ports
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Declare auxiliary variables with eq or const when needed for complex expressions
Use assign only for implementation outputs or auxiliary variables
Avoid AADL property names in AGREE expressions unless exposed as ports or constants
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Declare helper variables with appropriate types (int, real, bool)
Use if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE expressions
Ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax: assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions
Do not use invalid AGREE type names (use int, real, bool instead)
